preview_text:
The model rates Netherlands as a modest favorite at 36% to win, with Japan trailing at 33%. The gap is narrow enough that the outcome remains genuinely uncertain; the underdog holds a realistic chance of either victory or a draw.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects a competitive fixture. The model assigns 32% to a draw, making that outcome nearly as likely as either team's outright win. No single result dominates the forecast; all three paths carry meaningful probability. The match profiles as close and unpredictable.

The sportsbook consensus diverges notably from the model. Books price Netherlands at 47% to win—11 percentage points higher than the model—while trimming Japan to 26%, some 7 points below the model's estimate. Books also lean away from a draw (26% versus the model's 32%). The dashboard does not adjudicate between these two sources; the gap suggests material disagreement on the balance of play.
